pi_items.source: Task description specifies 'Create a new PostGIS data store named postgis_natural_earth in the cite workspace'. Verified against task.json metadata field and export_result.sh store name matching logic.

Natural Earth dataset documentation and setup_geoserver.sh line 114 confirms PostGIS table is named 'ne_countries'. Task description specifies publishing the 'ne_countries' table. Verified against evidence_docs/README.md line 70 showing PostGIS table 'ne_countries' in the gis database.

Task description explicitly states 'in the cite workspace'. 'cite' is a standard GeoServer demo workspace that comes with CITE (Compliance Interoperability Test Engine) sample data. Verified against GeoServer 2.28.x documentation showing 'cite' as one of 8 default demo workspaces.

Evidence_docs/README.md line 32 shows 'inflating: /home/ga/natural_earth/ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp' from Natural Earth download. Confirmed in setup_geoserver.sh line 113 and task setup scripts. This is the official Natural Earth 110m scale countries shapefile.

Evidence_docs/README.md line 9 explicitly states 'Real Data: Natural Earth 110m shapefiles (countries, populated places, rivers, lakes)'. Natural Earth is a public domain geospatial dataset from naturalearthdata.com. Download URLs shown in setup logs (line 30-42 of README) reference official Natural Earth source.
Task description states 'use hostname gs-postgis'. Confirmed in setup_geoserver.sh line 146 and docker-compose.yml container name 'gs-postgis'. This is the Docker container hostname.
Task description specifies 'port 5432'. Confirmed in docker-compose.yml line 14 port mapping and setup_geoserver.sh line 147. Standard PostgreSQL port.
Task description and setup_geoserver.sh line 100 specify database 'gis'. Confirmed in docker-compose.yml line 9 POSTGRES_DB=gis.
Task description specifies 'user geoserver'. Confirmed in docker-compose.yml line 10 POSTGRES_USER=geoserver and setup_geoserver.sh line 100.
Task description specifies 'password geoserver123'. Confirmed in docker-compose.yml line 11 POSTGRES_PASSWORD=geoserver123 and setup_geoserver.sh line 100.
Task description and docker-compose.yml line 41 port mapping. Confirmed in setup_geoserver.sh line 334.
Task description and docker-compose.yml line 32 GEOSERVER_ADMIN_USER=admin. Confirmed in setup_geoserver.sh line 333 and task_utils.sh line 7.
Task description and docker-compose.yml line 33 GEOSERVER_ADMIN_PASSWORD=Admin123!. Confirmed in setup_geoserver.sh line 334 and task_utils.sh line 7.
Docker-compose.yml line 25 specifies image kartoza/geoserver:2.25.2. Confirmed in evidence_docs/README.md line 8.
Docker-compose.yml line 5 specifies image kartoza/postgis:15-3.3. Confirmed in evidence_docs/README.md line 8.

privileged_info_summary: The publish_shapefile_layer task uses the real, publicly available Natural Earth 110m countries dataset (ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp). The metadata claims the expected layer name is 'ne_countries', the data store name is 'postgis_natural_earth', and the workspace is 'cite'. All metadata values are verified: ne_countries is the actual PostGIS table name created from the shapefile; postgis_natural_earth is the specified store name in the task description; cite is a standard GeoServer demo workspace. The task operates on authentic Natural Earth geospatial data downloaded from naturalearthdata.com during environment setup, containing country polygon features at 1:110m scale. GeoServer 2.25.2 and PostGIS 15 (with PostGIS extension 3.3) are the supporting infrastructure.
